title: SYNTHESIS OF IMIDAZOLINE COMPLEXES OF PETROLEUM ACIDS, RESEARCH AND APPLICATION OF THEIR PETROLEUM-COLLECTING AND DISPERSING, ELECTRICAL CONDUCTIVITY PROPERTIES
author: Mamedova N.
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: This article presents techniques for safeguarding water bodies in the event of oil spills at underwater crossings of both stationary and mobile oil pipelines. Additionally, a device for collecting oil beneath the surface of ice is introduced. Their technical description and operational principle are provided. Research has confirmed that the oil-holding capacity of the sorbent remains constant regardless of the length of time it spends in the water 1. The synthesized product was found to have high surface activity at the water-air interface, as determined by tensiometric measurements. Additionally, its colloidal-chemical parameters were calculated. Lab tests have demonstrated that both the reagent itself and its 5% aqueous solution possess a significant ability to collect oil.

keywords: oil collection, petroleum acid amidoamines, amino ester, surface activity.
